Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides a transparent, low-latency electrical path for the high-speed multimedia data stream (e.g., TMDS, DisplayPort data lanes) from the media source to the display, ensuring the video payload is delivered untouched.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Media source high-speed video output pins.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Display device high-speed video input pins.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Directly routes high-speed differential pairs through impedance-matched traces, buffers, or redriver ICs to maintain signal integrity without processing or altering the video data.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Intercepts the low-speed communication bus (e.g., DDC/CI for HDMI/DisplayPort, I2C, or AUX CH) used for device identification and capability exchange. It emulates a display by responding with spoofed EDID/DisplayID data stored in non-volatile memory.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Media source low-speed bus pins (SCL, SDA for I2C).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Onboard Non-Volatile Memory block and Central Processing Unit.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A bus switch or multiplexer initially connects the source to the real display for initial detection, then isolates it. A microcontroller or dedicated I2C/aux channel controller acts as a slave device on the bus, intercepting read requests from the source and fetching data from the memory block to send spoofed replies.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Stores customized display profiles (e.g., EDID binaries, resolution tables, vendor IDs) that define the emulated display's capabilities. Retains data when powered off.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Low-Speed Bus Intercept & Emulation Unit (for read operations).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Implemented using an EEPROM or Flash memory chip. The CPU can write new profiles via an SPI or I2C interface. The Low-Speed Bus Intercept unit reads from this memory directly or via a buffer when emulating the display for the source.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Manages system data flow, coordinates modules, handles user configuration updates, and triggers handshake re-negotiation. It listens for user commands and updates the memory block accordingly.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: External Communication Interface, Power Arbitration Network status.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Non-Volatile Memory Block, Connection Detection Signal Toggle, Low-Speed Bus Intercept & Emulation Unit.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A microcontroller or FPGA soft-core. It monitors the external interface (e.g., USB). Upon receiving a new profile, it writes it to the memory block. It then electronically toggles the Hot Plug Detect (HPD) or similar signal to force the media source to restart the handshake and read the new profile from memory.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides a user-accessible channel (e.g., USB, UART over micro-USB, Bluetooth) to upload new display profiles, update firmware, or configure system parameters.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A physical connector and corresponding transceiver IC (e.g., USB-UART bridge, USB controller). Converts user data from the external interface into a serial protocol (e.g., UART, SPI) understood by the CPU for processing.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Manages system power by seamlessly selecting and regulating the primary power source between the power provided by the video cable (e.g., +5V from HDMI) and an external auxiliary power source (e.g., USB-C PD, barrel jack). Provides stable, clean power to all logic circuits.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Video cable power pin (e.g., HDMI +5V), Auxiliary power input connector.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Uses power path controllers, ideal diodes, or MOSFET-based OR-ing circuits to automatically select the higher or preferred voltage source. Includes voltage regulators (LDOs or switching regulators) to generate required logic voltages (e.g., 3.3V, 1.8V). Ensures uninterrupted operation during source switching.
